title: Massive God-File and Overlong Functions in doctor.ts
description: doctor.ts is an 8696-line god-file packing report generation (runDoctorReport), three repair/mutation runners, and dozens of complex low-level inspectors (e.g. inspectL2ManagedBlockDrift spanning lines 3250-4012, over 750 lines). Highly regression-prone, slow to analyze, hard to test in isolation.
fix_direction: Decompose into a doctor/ directory; extract each validator/inspector into its own module (managed-block-drift.ts, orphan-demote.ts, ledger-invariant.ts) behind a consolidated orchestrator.

title: Boilerplate Copy-Paste Installation Functions for Skills
description: Identical boilerplate install functions exist for all 7 active Skills (installFabricArchiveSkill, installFabricReviewSkill, installFabricImportSkill, installFabricSyncSkill, installFabricStoreSkill, installFabricAuditSkill, installFabricConnectSkill). Each manually reads a template, validates size, maps destinations, runs copyTextIdempotent, and logs. Violates DRY and risks drift when adding skills.
fix_direction: Use a single data-driven helper installFabricSkill(skillKey, templateRel, options) with skills represented as config objects in a metadata array.

title: Duplicated Process Liveness and Lock Reading Logic across Packages
description: isAlive(pid) and readLockState(projectRoot) are duplicated line-for-line between legacy-serve-lock-probe.ts (server) and serve-lock.ts (server-http-experimental). Redundant logic raises maintenance overhead and risks divergence if lock behavior changes.
fix_direction: Consolidate these OS/process utilities in the shared package (e.g. @fenglimg/fabric-shared/node lock-utils.ts) and import in both packages.

title: Circular Dependency Cycle in Doctor Services
description: Circular dependency between doctor.ts and doctor-cite-coverage.ts: doctor.ts imports from doctor-cite-coverage.js (lines 8203, 8223) while doctor-cite-coverage.ts imports inspectL1BootstrapSnapshotDrift and normalizePath back from doctor.js (line 68). Resolved at call-time but tightly couples the domains and complicates testing/tree-shaking.
fix_direction: Move shared utilities (inspectL1BootstrapSnapshotDrift, normalizePath) and core types into a common doctor-utils.ts both modules import, eliminating the cycle.

title: Cloned Dead Code and Unused Imports in doctor-cite-coverage.ts
description: doctor-cite-coverage.ts has a large dead-code import block copied verbatim when split out of doctor.ts, leaving unused imports (execFileSync, spawnSync from node:child_process; mkdirSync, readdirSync from node:fs; access, appendFile, mkdir, readFile, rename, unlink, writeFile from node:fs/promises; homedir from node:os; Script from node:vm).
fix_direction: Prune the unused imports and Node module dependencies from the file header.

title: Brittle Class Identity Checks in HTTP Server Error Handling
description: The HTTP server error handler relies on error instanceof FabricError. Crossing package/bundle boundaries in a monorepo can make instanceof fail due to multiple class symbol instances. The CLI resolved this with structural duck-typing (hasActionHint), but the HTTP server still uses fragile class identity matching.
fix_direction: Migrate HTTP server error normalization to the same structural type-guard (hasActionHint) exported by the shared package.

title: Inconsistent and Bloated Error/Return Contracts in Doctor APIs
description: The doctor service surfaces three report types (DoctorReport, DoctorFixReport, DoctorApplyLintReport) with slightly different fields representing the same concept using disparate keys (fixable_errors vs fixed vs mutations; manual_errors vs remaining_manual_errors), and all duplicate the full raw report: DoctorReport inside themselves — API bloat and high integration complexity.
fix_direction: Consolidate around a single layered schema (unified DoctorReport with an optional remedy/mutation transaction block) rather than three divergent envelopes.

title: Unclear and Cryptic Core Type Names in Agents Metadata
description: Core counter schemas use cryptic acronyms. In AgentsMetaCountersEnvelope, counters sit under KP and KT (personal/team layers) using keys MOD, DEC, GLD, PIT, PRO (models, decisions, guidelines, pitfalls, processes), making the code harder to read/maintain for new developers.
fix_direction: Rename interface keys to descriptive names (personal/team instead of KP/KT; models/decisions/... instead of MOD/DEC) or provide clear documentation/mappings in the type files.
